title: Jamaican pumpkin soup
subtitle: Thick, spiced pumpkin and ground provisions soup
excerpt: Pumpkin and sweet potato simmered with scotch bonnet, spring onion, thyme, and allspice until soft, then blended to a smooth, velvety soup and finished with coconut milk. The scotch bonnet heat cuts through the sweetness of the pumpkin; the allspice adds a deep background warmth. Jamaican pumpkin soup is one of the Saturday soup trinity alongside chicken soup and pepperpot.
sourceNotes: Pumpkin soup is one of Jamaica's three classic Saturday soups, made throughout the island and the diaspora. The Jamaican pumpkin used is calabaza (West Indian pumpkin), a large green-skinned squash with vibrant orange flesh that is available in Caribbean food shops in Britain. Butternut squash is the nearest substitute.

content.text: Jamaican pumpkin (calabaza) has a drier, more intensely flavoured flesh than butternut squash, and the colour is deeper orange. If you find it in a Caribbean food shop, it makes a noticeably better soup. If using butternut squash, the result is still very good but slightly less sweet and less colourful.

The scotch bonnet should be added whole and removed before blending, unless you want a very spicy soup. The heat of a whole scotch bonnet simmered for 30 minutes infuses a gentle warmth that complements rather than dominates the pumpkin. For a spicier version, slice and deseed it and leave it in.

items.name: Pumpkin
items.prepNote: calabaza or butternut squash, peeled and diced

content.text: Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a large pot over medium heat. Add the {{onion}} and cook for 5 minutes. Add the {{garlic}}, {{spring-onion}}, {{thyme}}, {{allspice}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} and cook for 2 minutes. Add the {{pumpkin}}, {{sweet-potato}}, and {{scotch-bonnet}} (whole). Pour in 800 ml of water and bring to a boil. Reduce to a simmer, cover, and cook for 25 to 30 minutes until the vegetables are completely soft. Remove the scotch bonnet and thyme sprigs. Blend until completely smooth with a stick blender. Add the {{coconut-milk}}, stir well, and reheat gently. Taste and adjust seasoning.

items.fix: Simmer uncovered after blending to reduce to the desired thickness. The sweet potato starch thickens the soup as it cooks
items.cause: Too much water added, or not enough starchy vegetables

items.fix: Calabaza has a much deeper colour. The flavour and texture are the same; only the colour differs significantly
items.cause: Using butternut squash rather than calabaza pumpkin
items.symptom: Soup very pale and yellow rather than deep orange

items.fix: Add a squeeze of lime juice to balance the sweetness. A pinch more salt also cuts the sweet note

content.text: Pumpkin soup is Saturday soup alongside chicken soup and pepperpot in the Jamaican soup tradition. The three soups show the different moods of Jamaican Saturday cooking. Chicken soup is restorative and simple. Pepperpot is dark and meaty. Pumpkin soup is smooth and comforting. All three are made in the morning and served at midday. All three are thick and robust, not the delicate consommé-style soups of European tradition. The coconut milk sets the Jamaican version apart from the plainer Eastern Caribbean pumpkin soups.
